metadata.falPrompt:
Create a full-screen 2D HD pixel-hybrid tactical RPG monster status sheet UI background.

The image must be a clean empty interface template, not a completed character sheet. It should be designed for a game engine to render all monster names, numbers, ability names, icons, percentages, rewards, and descriptions later.

Resolution: 1536x1024.
Perspective: perfectly flat orthographic front-facing UI asset.
Style: dark medieval Italian manuscript, 13th-century illuminated parchment, ornate but readable, inspired by Dante-era Italy and tactical RPG menus.
Color palette: aged parchment, dark walnut wood, muted gold filigree, ink brown, bone ivory, subtle crimson accents. No neon colors.

CRITICAL RULES:
- Do not include sample monster names.
- Do not include sample numbers.
- Do not include fake values like 000, ---, 10%, Lv. 1, etc.
- Do not include ability names.
- Do not include equipment names.
- Do not include elemental fantasy labels like Ice, Thunder, Fire, Dark.
- Do not include hardcoded stat names inside the art unless specifically listed below as section headers.
- Do not include character art or monster art.
- Do not include random letters or AI-generated gibberish.
- Leave clean blank areas for the game engine to render all text.

Layout:
The interface is a wide parchment ledger divided into clean framed panels.

TOP ROW, about 45% height:
1. LEFT PANEL: Monster Profile
- Ornate rectangular portrait frame for monster sprite or monster illustration.
- Empty parchment interior.
- Small blank title strip beneath the portrait for monster name and family.
- No text inside the strip except optional section header “Profile”.

2. CENTER PANEL: Core Stats
- Large clean data panel with two neat vertical columns.
- Include faint empty value boxes or horizontal guide lines.
- No numbers.
- No baked stat labels except optional section header “Stats”.
- Leave enough room for engine-rendered stats such as HP, MP, CT, STR, SPD, INT, FTH, POW, DEF, MOV, JMP, EXP.

3. RIGHT PANEL: Status Resist
- Narrow vertical panel for six status resistance rows.
- Each row should have a small empty icon socket on the left, a short blank label space, and a small blank value space on the right.
- No status names baked in.
- No percentages baked in.
- Section header may read “Resist”.

BOTTOM ROW, about 55% height:
4. LEFT LARGE PANEL: Abilities
- Four wide ability slots in a 2x2 grid.
- Each ability slot has a small square icon socket on the left and a large blank text area for ability name/effect.
- No sample text.
- Section header may read “Abilities”.

5. CENTER SMALL PANEL: Traits
- Compact vertical list area for monster traits, AI tags, weaknesses, or behavior markers.
- 4 to 6 small rows with tiny icon sockets and blank text lanes.
- Section header may read “Traits”.

6. RIGHT PANEL: Rewards
- Clean panel for rewards and drops.
- Include several empty square item sockets and blank text lines.
- Section header may read “Rewards”.
- No coin icons with values, no sample item names, no numbers.

Visual details:
- Thin dark ink panel borders with gold corner ornaments.
- Parchment texture should be rich but low contrast behind text areas.
- Subtle fresco watermark in the background, very faded, not distracting.
- Use consistent spacing and alignment.
- Text areas must be clean and readable when dark engine text is placed on top.
- Avoid clutter.
- Avoid baked-in grid numbers or placeholder digits.
- Avoid modern sci-fi UI shapes.
- No drop shadows crossing text areas.
- No perspective tilt.
- No blur.
- No screenshot framing.
- No black background outside the UI.

The final image should look like a polished empty monster status sheet background from a high-quality 2D HD tactical RPG, ready for Godot to render monster data dynamically on top., 16-bit pixel art, SNES-era detailed sprites, rich palette
